name: runbaypty
description: OS-managed daemon that owns PTY sessions so they survive any client rebuild, crash, or quit — with sequence-numbered zero-gap replay, a documented binary protocol over Unix socket + WebSocket, a Go SDK, and zero-downtime daemon upgrades via fd handover.
author: khanakia

positioning.audience: developers building terminal-adjacent tools: AI-agent orchestrators, web IDEs, desktop apps, CI systems
differentiators: zero-downtime daemon upgrades via SCM_RIGHTS fd handover — not even tmux survives its own upgrade
documented additive-only binary protocol + first-class Go SDK
agent-era primitives: silence/activity events, OSC 133 command tracking, server-side WATCH, write-lock handoff

brand.note: branding phase deliberately skipped per user instruction 2026-07-10; re-run satl-brand-kit later if wanted
